title: Display CPU limit in `podman stats`
body:
### Feature request description

Display CPU limit in `podman stats` the same way limit is displayed for memory usage.



### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

Limits can be imposed on a container, for example:
- https://github.com/compose-spec/compose-spec/blob/master/deploy.md#cpus
- https://github.com/compose-spec/compose-spec/blob/master/spec.md#cpu_count

I have tested, and indeed `podman-compose` restricts a container to the deploy.resources.limits.cpus set, but `podman stats` does not show CPU limits, even though it shows deploy.resources.limits.memory.

comments.body:
How should this be displayed? There are a several different cpu specific limits that do different things.
```
$ podman run --help | grep -- --cpu
      --cpu-period uint                          Limit the CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) period
      --cpu-quota int                            Limit the CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) quota
      --cpu-rt-period uint                       Limit the CPU real-time period in microseconds
      --cpu-rt-runtime int                       Limit the CPU real-time runtime in microseconds
  -c, --cpu-shares uint                          CPU shares (relative weight)
      --cpus float                               Number of CPUs. The default is 0.000 which means no limit
      --cpuset-cpus string                       CPUs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)
      --cpuset-mems string                       Memory nodes (MEMs) in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1). Only effective on NUMA systems.

```

I would start with something close to the current display of memory limit. Calculate the % total cpu allowed to a container based on cpus and cpu-shares and display it beside CPU. For example:
```
ID            NAME                  CPU % / LIMIT         MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO           PIDS        CPU TIME       AVG CPU %
``` 

With CPU the same value as currently, the total CPUs used on the host, and LIMIT as the total percent of CPUs that can be used. So for example if the host has 8 cores, cpus is set to 0.25, and a multithreaded process in the container is using 100% of one core and 50% of another, CPU % would display 0.19 and LIMIT would display 0.25.

First I don't think we should change the default display order. I fear people might depend on the current order and try to string parse which is not a good idea in general but I rather not change it unless absolutely necessary.

> So for example if the host has 8 cores, cpus is set to 0.25, and a multithreaded process in the container is using 100% of one core and 50% of another, CPU % would display 0.19 and LIMIT would display 0.25.

Wouldn't that totally break the current user expectation if we show percentages based on the limit and not total.

> Wouldn't that totally break the current user expectation if we show percentages based on the limit and not total.

Not if we show both, CPU %, and CPU limit.

I have a container with these limits in the compose file:
```
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
          cpus: '0.0625'
```

I run it with `podman-compose`, and `podman stats` shows this. "CPU %" varies between 0 and 6.25%, which is exactly what I expect (half of a core on an 8 core machine). But in a configuration with multiple containers with different limit, it'd be difficult to remember which one has what limit. Having an extra column showing that limit would be really useful, the same way seeing the memory limit here is very helpful.
 ```
ID            NAME            CPU %       MEM USAGE / LIMIT  MEM %       NET IO      BLOCK IO           PIDS        CPU TIME    AVG CPU %
d404beebb3a4  pc-bug_small_1  6.22%       1.65GB / 2.147GB   76.85%      0B / 0B     285.1MB / 801.6MB  5           8.250014s   3.68%
```

> we should change the default display order. I fear people might depend on the current order and try to string parse

Could we add it as an option? Something like `-e` for enhanced, with a warning that the format is changing all the time, so we don't need to add new options every time a new column is added.
